You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions.
Tat Tvam Asi — Thou art That.

"The Self is not born, nor does it die. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain."
"Awake, arise, and learn by approaching the exalted ones. The path is sharp as the edge of a razor, difficult to traverse — so say the wise."
"Om iti ekaksharam brahma — Om, this syllable is all this. All that is past, present, and future — all of this is just Om."

"Brahman is reality, knowledge, and infinity. One who knows Brahman as existing in the supreme space within the heart becomes one with Brahman and enjoys all desires simultaneously with the all-knowing Brahman."
"The Self cannot be pierced by weapons or burned by fire; water cannot wet it, nor can the wind dry it."
"Two birds, inseparable companions, perch on the same tree. One eats the fruits, the other looks on without eating."
"That which is the finest essence — this whole world has that as its soul. That is Reality. That is Atman. That art thou, Shvetaketu."
"The wise one who knows the Self as bodiless within the bodies, as unchanging among changing things, as great and omnipresent — that wise one grieves not."
"There are two selves — the separate ego and the indivisible Atman. When one rises above I and me and mine, the Atman is revealed as one's real Self."
